title: Removing from Lists
description: Learn to use pop() to remove items and length to count them.

preDialogues.name: Manu
preDialogues.content: You've learned how to add items to lists. Now let's learn how to remove them!

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: How do I take items out of a list?

preDialogues.name: Manu
preDialogues.content: Use the pop() method! It removes and returns the last item. If you have [1, 2, 3] and call pop(myList), you get 3 and the list becomes [1, 2].

preDialogues.name: Manu
preDialogues.content: Let's try it! Create a list with several numbers, then use pop() to remove the last one.

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: So pop() both removes it AND gives me the value?

preDialogues.name: Manu
preDialogues.content: Exactly! You can store it in a variable: poppedItem = pop(myList)

objectives.description: Use pop() to remove an item

postDialogues.name: Manu
postDialogues.content: Perfect! You've removed an item from your list. Now let's learn about list length.

preDialogues.name: Manu
preDialogues.content: To find out how many items are in your list, use the len() property!

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: So if I have [1, 2, 3, 4], the length is 4?

preDialogues.name: Manu
preDialogues.content: Exactly! Use len(myList) to get the count. Try creating a list and checking its length.

objectives.description: Use len() to get list size

postDialogues.name: Manu
postDialogues.content: Great! Length is very useful for knowing how much data you have.

preDialogues.name: Manu
preDialogues.content: Here's a practical use: create a harvest queue! Add items, then pop them off as you process each harvest.

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: Like a to-do list that I check off?

preDialogues.name: Manu
preDialogues.content: Exactly! Harvest 3 wheat, add them to a list, then pop items off. Try it!

postDialogues.name: Manu
postDialogues.content: Excellent! You're managing data like a pro programmer! Next: calculations!

postDialogues.name: Manu
postDialogues.content: You'll learn to calculate totals, averages, and more with the sum() function!
